docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Rizwan's personal website

iRizwan

I write about building business-aware, data-informed, production-ready software. This is a personal site about who I am, what I am learning, and the kind of work I want to do in public.

Who I Am What I Write About FDE Thread

docs.title: Who I am
docs.text:
I am Rizwan. I work across business analysis and software delivery while deepening my data science and cloud deployment skills.

This site is personal, but it has a clear theme: how useful technical work gets from an idea into something people can actually use. That means understanding the business context, asking better questions of the data, building maintainable software, and learning how production systems are deployed and supported.

docs.title: Why this site exists
docs.text: I use iRizwan to write about the overlap between business, data, software, and production delivery. Some of the writing will be educational. Some of it will be practical notes from learning, building, and working through technical problems. The common thread is the same: useful work should not stop at a document, dashboard, notebook, or prototype if it can become a reliable system.

docs.title: What I write about
docs.text:
The site focuses on a few connected topics:

  • business analysis and requirements
  • data science and practical forecasting
  • software engineering for maintainable delivery
  • cloud deployment with AWS
  • open-source learning and public notes
  • forward deployment engineering

docs.title: Forward deployment engineering
docs.text:
Forward deployment engineering is one way to describe the mix of skills I am writing about. It is the label I use for work that starts with a business problem, uses data honestly, and ends with software that can run in production.

That is why the topic matters here. It gives a name to the kind of work I want to understand and explain, but it does not replace the personal side of the site.

docs.title: What it means to me
docs.text: I want the site to feel like a public notebook for learning, not a generic marketing page. The personal layer matters because colleagues, friends, or future collaborators should be able to see who I am first, then understand the technical thread underneath.

docs.title: What I am building toward
docs.text:
The direction of this site is to become a personal record of learning, building, and explaining how business-aware software gets made. The topics I expect to write about here include:

  • business analysis and requirements
  • data science and practical forecasting
  • software engineering for maintainable delivery
  • cloud deployment with AWS
  • open-source learning and public notes
  • the forward deployment engineer skill set
